text:
There was a squirrel who loved to gather acorns. Every day, the squirrel would scamper around the forest searching for new places to collect nuts. He was particularly fond of oaks.

One day as the squirrel was munching on some acorns he collected, he stumbled upon an old tree trunk hidden by foliage. Beneath it was an unusual object: a beautifully carved chestnut loaf. Curious, the squirrel picked it up, admiring its craftsmanship.

He scampered back to his burrow, curious about this unusual find. Upon opening it, the squirrel discovered a cookbook titled "How to bake a loaf-shaped nut." The owl who owned it had spent years perfecting their recipes.

With excitement, the squirrel opened the cookbook. There were pages and pages of delicious recipes that involved nothing but nuts. The loaf sounded divine.

The squirrel set to work, gathering a variety of nuts: chestnuts, hazelnuts, pecans, walnuts, even dried cranberries. He mixed
